anti_claim: This standard governs a public Batch-4 source-open capsule and exact copied non-secret macro source bodies. It does not solve Erdos #257, authorize publication or release, certify benchmark performance, dispatch providers, operate a live sandbox, export live Codex or metabolism state, mutate source, or prove whole-system correctness.
kind_name: Batch 4 proof, authority, and runtime capsule

allowed_public_inputs: public mechanism ids and source-module ids
public negative-case ids and stable error codes
exact copied non-secret macro source modules
source refs, digests, line counts, and required anchors

standard_payload.anti_purpose: Fourteen disconnected registry claims or a source-free receipt that hides the macro bodies and relies on prose instead of digest, anchor, negative-case, and authority-ceiling checks.
failure_modes: mechanism count drifts from the 14 Batch-4 targets
source module count drifts from 19 without an updated manifest and tests
copied source module digest does not match macro source digest
copied source module lacks required proof, authority, runtime, or diagnostic anchors
Erdos certificate-kernel scope is overread as a solution or publication result
VeriSoftBench diagnostic rows are overread as official benchmark scores
reasoning authority grant rows authorize live launch, model dispatch, provider dispatch, or runtime execution
closeout executor rows are overread as publication or release approval
Codex driver or idle heartbeat rows export live session state
metabolism rows export live database state
receipts include copied body text, provider/browser/session payloads, credentials, or private runtime state
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe composite organ that imports Batch-4 proof-control, machine-verified math, authority-planning, closeout, Codex runtime diagnostic, bitemporal coordination, taskpolicy, and context-yield mechanisms as exact copied macro source bodies plus bounded public exercises.
standard_payload.teleology: Let a cold reader inspect and rerun the Batch-4 evidence capsule without confusing source-open anchors with live runtime authority, publication authority, or benchmark/proof claims.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; accepted organ registry and receipt anchors bound the source row but do not grant release, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness authority.

validation_rules: require exactly 19 copied macro source modules
verify source-to-target digest equality and required anchors for every copied source module

require stable negative-case codes for proof-control, foundry, VeriSoftBench, Erdos overclaim, packet corruption, authority grants, forward integration, closeout, Codex diagnostics, bitemporal claims, taskpolicy, and context-yield attribution
keep launch_authorized, model_dispatch, provider_dispatch, runtime_execution, live Codex dispatch, source mutation, official leaderboard submission, publication, and release authority false
keep copied source bodies under source_modules only; receipts may expose refs, digests, anchors, counts, verdicts, and omission receipts but not body text
preserve Erdos #257 anti-claims: certificate-kernel and static-scan scope only, not a solution and not publication authority

author.purpose: create a valid Batch 4 proof, authority, and runtime capsule instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Batch 4 proof, authority, and runtime capsule without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_batch4_proof_authority_runtime and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections
